what: rkagi: R Client for Kagi APIs
title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: Installation moment via github: use rkagi need active Kagi account API subscription. Kagi account Sign https://kagi.com. API available paid Kagi accounts (Starter, Standard, Professional). need enable billing; free trial accounts use API. Enable API access Go Kagi Account Dashboard. API Access, generate new API key. Copy key safe place. Store key securely Use keyring package save key system keychain: prompted enter key. stored securely system keychain. Use key rkagi creating connection, resolve key lazily: key looked request performed, never stored plain text. Respect limits billing endpoint (Search, Enrich, Summarizer) pricing rate limits. API usage deducts Kagi balance. See https://help.kagi.com/kagi/api/ details. Connections Represent link API. Created new_kagi_connection(base_url, endpoint, api_key). Usually constructed automatically higher-level *_once() helpers. Requests Lightweight S3 objects describe want send API (e.g. kagi_search, kagi_enrich, kagi_summarize). Results Typed S3 objects wrap API response, including raw JSON, metadata, parsed tibble summary (e.g. kagi_search_results, kagi_enrich_results, kagi_summarize_results). Helpers Functions make life easier, build_kagi_query() compose advanced search queries, open_kagi_query() preview queries browser. rkagi provides helpers compose complex search queries safely. can combine free-text terms structured operators: filetype: – restrict file extension (vector values combined ) site: – restrict domain (vector values combined ) inurl: – require substrings URL (values combined ) intitle: – require words page title (values combined ) Create connection prepared search, perform . Results typed S3 objects print nicely provide helpers extract hits, related queries, metadata. kagi_search_results object contains: json: raw JSON string meta: list response metadata (e.g. latency, node, API balance) data: tibble search results (NULL empty) Use extractor helpers (kagi_hits(), kagi_related(), kagi_meta()) rather accessing slots directly. Enrich endpoint returns higher-signal results specialized indices. workflow mirrors Search: request → perform → helpers. kagi_enrich_results object contains: json: raw JSON string meta: list response metadata (e.g. latency, node, API balance) data: tibble enrichment hits (NULL empty) Use extractor helper enrich_extract() rather accessing slots directly. Universal Summarizer can condense either URL (GET) raw text (POST). Results returned S3 objects containing summary, tokens, meta. kagi_summarize_results object contains: summary: summarizer output text tokens: token usage reported API meta: list response metadata (latency, node, API balance) raw_json: raw JSON string (written disk path provided) HTTP errors converted informative R errors using httr2::req_error(). API-provided messages (present) preserved thrown conditions. Consider defining custom condition classes allow tryCatch() users. Respect Retry-headers consider simple backoff 429/503 responses. Avoid hammering endpoints; cache raw JSON disk feasible using path argument *_perform().
code: if (requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE) == FALSE) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("rkrug/rkagi") keyring::key_set("API_kagi") conn <- new_kagi_connection(   endpoint = "search",   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) library(rkagi)  q <- build_kagi_query(   query    = 'biodiversity "annual report"',   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site     = c("example.com", "gov"),   inurl    = c("2024", "report"),   intitle  = "summary" )  q #> biodiversity "annual report" (filetype:pdf OR filetype:docx) (site:example.com OR site:gov) inurl:2024 inurl:report intitle:summary  # Preview directly in your browser open_kagi_query(q) conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search")  # api_key resolved lazily  s <- new_kagi_search(conn, q = q, limit = 5) s                                # prints the request  res <- kagi_perform(s, path = tempfile(fileext = ".json"))  # optional: save raw JSON res                               # prints summary (hits, related, timing)  hits    <- kagi_hits(res)         # tibble of t == 0 results related <- kagi_related(res)      # character vector of t == 1 related queries meta    <- kagi_meta(res)         # list of { id, node, ms, api_balance } res <- kagi_search_once("openalex api", limit = 3) kagi_hits(res) conn_e <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "enrich")  # or 'enrich/web' depending on API flavor req_e  <- new_kagi_enrich(conn_e, q = q, limit = 5) res_e  <- kagi_perform(req_e, path = tempfile(fileext = ".json"))  res_e                          # print summary enrich_items <- enrich_extract(res_e)  # tibble of enrichment hits res_e <- kagi_enrich_once("open data", limit = 5) enrich_extract(res_e) conn_s <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "summarize")  # --- URL mode --- req_s <- new_summarize_request(   conn_s,   url             = "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZSRHeXYDLko",   engine          = "muriel",   summary_type    = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  res_s <- summarize_perform(req_s, path = tempfile(fileext = ".json"))  summarizer_output(res_s)  # summary text summarizer_tokens(res_s)  # token usage summarizer_meta(res_s)    # metadata # --- Text mode --- req_s2 <- new_summarize_request(   conn_s,   text            = "Paste long text here ...",   engine          = "cecil",   summary_type    = "takeaway",   target_language = "EN",   cache           = FALSE )  res_s2 <- summarize_perform(req_s2) summarizer_output(res_s2) res <- kagi_summarize_once(   url = "https://www.example.com/long-article",   engine = "muriel",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" ) summarizer_output(res) sessionInfo()

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: Installation moment via github:
code: if (requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE) == FALSE) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("rkrug/rkagi")

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: use rkagi need active Kagi account API subscription. Kagi account Sign https://kagi.com. API available paid Kagi accounts (Starter, Standard, Professional). need enable billing; free trial accounts use API. Enable API access Go Kagi Account Dashboard. API Access, generate new API key. Copy key safe place. Store key securely Use keyring package save key system keychain: prompted enter key. stored securely system keychain. Use key rkagi creating connection, resolve key lazily: key looked request performed, never stored plain text. Respect limits billing endpoint (Search, Enrich, Summarizer) pricing rate limits. API usage deducts Kagi balance. See https://help.kagi.com/kagi/api/ details.
code: keyring::key_set("API_kagi") conn <- new_kagi_connection(   endpoint = "search",   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: Connections Represent link API. Created new_kagi_connection(base_url, endpoint, api_key). Usually constructed automatically higher-level *_once() helpers. Requests Lightweight S3 objects describe want send API (e.g. kagi_search, kagi_enrich, kagi_summarize). Results Typed S3 objects wrap API response, including raw JSON, metadata, parsed tibble summary (e.g. kagi_search_results, kagi_enrich_results, kagi_summarize_results). Helpers Functions make life easier, build_kagi_query() compose advanced search queries, open_kagi_query() preview queries browser.

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: rkagi provides helpers compose complex search queries safely. can combine free-text terms structured operators: filetype: – restrict file extension (vector values combined ) site: – restrict domain (vector values combined ) inurl: – require substrings URL (values combined ) intitle: – require words page title (values combined )
code: library(rkagi)  q <- build_kagi_query(   query    = 'biodiversity "annual report"',   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site     = c("example.com", "gov"),   inurl    = c("2024", "report"),   intitle  = "summary" )  q #> biodiversity "annual report" (filetype:pdf OR filetype:docx) (site:example.com OR site:gov) inurl:2024 inurl:report intitle:summary  # Preview directly in your browser open_kagi_query(q)

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: Create connection prepared search, perform . Results typed S3 objects print nicely provide helpers extract hits, related queries, metadata. kagi_search_results object contains: json: raw JSON string meta: list response metadata (e.g. latency, node, API balance) data: tibble search results (NULL empty) Use extractor helpers (kagi_hits(), kagi_related(), kagi_meta()) rather accessing slots directly.
code: conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search")  # api_key resolved lazily  s <- new_kagi_search(conn, q = q, limit = 5) s                                # prints the request  res <- kagi_perform(s, path = tempfile(fileext = ".json"))  # optional: save raw JSON res                               # prints summary (hits, related, timing)  hits    <- kagi_hits(res)         # tibble of t == 0 results related <- kagi_related(res)      # character vector of t == 1 related queries meta    <- kagi_meta(res)         # list of { id, node, ms, api_balance } res <- kagi_search_once("openalex api", limit = 3) kagi_hits(res)

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: HTTP errors converted informative R errors using httr2::req_error(). API-provided messages (present) preserved thrown conditions. Consider defining custom condition classes allow tryCatch() users.

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide
text: Respect Retry-headers consider simple backoff 429/503 responses. Avoid hammering endpoints; cache raw JSON disk feasible using path argument *_perform().

title: rkagi Quickstart Guide

title: Kagi API Client for R
text: R client Kagi API.

title: Kagi API Client for R
text: rkagi provides lightweight R interface Kagi API, including: Search API — perform web searches advanced operators Enrich API — get higher-signal results specialized indices Universal Summarizer — summarize text URLs one call package follows rOpenSci style API clients: S3 classes connections, requests, results Extractor helpers work results tibbles text Secure API key handling keyring

title: Kagi API Client for R
code: # Install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("rkrug/rkagi")

title: Kagi API Client for R
text: need Kagi account API access (paid plan). Store API key securely system keychain: package resolve key request time :
code: # Run once to save your key in the keychain keyring::key_set("API_kagi") conn <- new_kagi_connection(   endpoint = "search",   api_key  = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )

title: Kagi API Client for R
code: library(rkagi)  # Build a query q <- build_kagi_query(   query    = 'biodiversity "annual report"',   filetype = "pdf",   site     = "example.com" )  # Perform a one-liner search res <- kagi_search_once(q, limit = 3) kagi_hits(res)

what: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
title: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
text: Construct one query strings Kagi Search API combining free-text terms structured operators filetype:, site:, inurl:, intitle:. Queries can either concatenated single string expanded Cartesian product combinations.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
code: build_kagi_query(   query,   filetype = NULL,   site = NULL,   inurl = NULL,   intitle = NULL,   expand = TRUE,   open_in_browser = FALSE )

title: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
text: query Character vector free-text query terms (required). can include quoted phrases boolean operators. filetype Optional character vector file type extensions (e.g. "pdf", "docx"). prefixed filetype:. site Optional character vector domains (e.g. "example.com", "gov"). prefixed site:. inurl Optional character vector URL substrings must present result URL. prefixed inurl:. intitle Optional character vector terms must appear page title. prefixed intitle:. expand Logical, default TRUE. TRUE, generate fully crossed set queries (Cartesian product combinations query, filetype, site, inurl, intitle). FALSE, concatenate arguments single combined query string. open_in_browser Logical, default FALSE. TRUE, generated query immediately opened default web browser via open_kagi_query() inspection.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
text: character vector query strings, suitable use q parameter kagi_search_once() kagi_enrich_once().

title: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
text: helper makes easy build reproducible, complex queries structured operators. Use expand = TRUE want possible combinations (useful systematic search contexts). Use expand = FALSE want single combined query.

title: Build a Kagi search query string — build_kagi_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single combined query build_kagi_query(   query = "biodiversity",   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = "example.com",   expand = FALSE )  # Expanded combinations build_kagi_query(   query = c("biodiversity", "ecosystem"),   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),   site = c("example.com", "gov"),   expand = TRUE )  # Immediately open in browser build_kagi_query("openalex api", site = "docs.openalex.org", open_in_browser = TRUE) } # }

what: Perform a Kagi enrichment in one call — kagi_enrich_once
title: Perform a Kagi enrichment in one call — kagi_enrich_once
text: high-level convenience wrapper around new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_enrich(), kagi_perform(). constructs connection, prepares enrichment request, performs single step.

title: Perform a Kagi enrichment in one call — kagi_enrich_once
code: kagi_enrich_once(   q,   limit,   api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),   base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",   path = NULL )

title: Perform a Kagi enrichment in one call — kagi_enrich_once
text: q Character scalar. enrichment query string. can include search operators filetype:pdf, site:example.com, boolean logic (, ), quoted phrases, etc. limit Optional integer. Maximum number enrichment results request. NULL, API return default number hits. api_key API key use authentication. default read KAGI_API_KEY environment variable. best practice, set Sys.setenv(KAGI_API_KEY="...") add ~/.Renviron. Advanced users can also pass function resolves key lazily (see new_kagi_connection()). base_url Base URL Kagi API. Defaults "https://kagi.com/api/v0". Override testing mock server future API version. path Optional file path. supplied, raw JSON response written path reproducibility debugging.

title: Perform a Kagi enrichment in one call — kagi_enrich_once
text: object class kagi_enrich_results. Key components include: data tibble enrichment hits. kagi_meta() List response metadata (latency, node, API balance).

title: Perform a Kagi enrichment in one call — kagi_enrich_once
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple one-liner res <- kagi_enrich_once("open data", limit = 3) enrich_extract(res)  # With a complex query q <- build_kagi_q(query = "biodiversity",                   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),                   site = c("example.com", "gov")) res <- kagi_enrich_once(q, limit = 5) enrich_extract(res) } # }

what: Perform a Kagi search in one call — kagi_search_once
title: Perform a Kagi search in one call — kagi_search_once
text: high-level convenience wrapper around new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_search(), kagi_perform(). constructs connection, prepares search request, performs single step.

title: Perform a Kagi search in one call — kagi_search_once
code: kagi_search_once(   q,   limit = NULL,   api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),   base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",   path = NULL )

title: Perform a Kagi search in one call — kagi_search_once
text: q Character scalar. search query string. can include search operators filetype:pdf, site:example.com, boolean logic (, ), quoted phrases, etc. limit Optional integer. Maximum number results request. NULL (default), API return default number hits. api_key API key use authentication. default read KAGI_API_KEY environment variable. best practice, set Sys.setenv(KAGI_API_KEY="...") add ~/.Renviron. Advanced users can also pass function resolves key lazily (see new_kagi_connection()). base_url Base URL Kagi API. Defaults "https://kagi.com/api/v0". Override testing mock server future API version. path Optional file path. supplied, raw JSON response written path reproducibility debugging.

title: Perform a Kagi search in one call — kagi_search_once
text: object class kagi_search_results. Use helper functions extract components: kagi_hits() Return tibble primary search hits. kagi_related() Character vector related queries. kagi_meta() List response metadata (latency, node, API balance).

title: Perform a Kagi search in one call — kagi_search_once
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple one-liner res <- kagi_search_once("openalex api", limit = 3) kagi_hits(res)  # With a complex query q <- build_kagi_q(query = "biodiversity",                   filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),                   site = c("example.com", "gov")) res <- kagi_search_once(q, limit = 5) kagi_hits(res) } # }

what: Perform a Kagi summarization in one call — kagi_summarize_once
title: Perform a Kagi summarization in one call — kagi_summarize_once
text: high-level convenience wrapper around new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_summarize(), kagi_perform(). constructs connection, prepares summarization request, performs single step.

title: Perform a Kagi summarization in one call — kagi_summarize_once
code: kagi_summarize_once(   url = NULL,   text = NULL,   engine = NULL,   summary_type = NULL,   target_language = NULL,   cache = NULL,   api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),   base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",   path = NULL )

title: Perform a Kagi summarization in one call — kagi_summarize_once
text: url Optional character scalar. URL summarized. Mutually exclusive text. provided, text takes precedence. text Optional character scalar. Raw text summarized. Mutually exclusive url. engine Optional character scalar. Summarizer engine use (e.g. "muriel", "cecil"). NULL, API default used. summary_type Optional character scalar. Type summary requested (e.g. "summary", "takeaway"). NULL, API default used. target_language Optional character scalar. Target language summary (ISO code "EN", "FR", "DE", etc.). NULL, API default used. cache Optional logical. Whether use cached summarizations API side. NULL, API default used. api_key API key use authentication. default read KAGI_API_KEY environment variable. best practice, set Sys.setenv(KAGI_API_KEY="...") add ~/.Renviron. Advanced users can also pass function resolves key lazily (see new_kagi_connection()). base_url Base URL Kagi API. Defaults "https://kagi.com/api/v0". Override testing mock server future API version. path Optional file path. supplied, raw JSON response written path reproducibility debugging.

title: Perform a Kagi summarization in one call — kagi_summarize_once
text: object class kagi_summarize_results. Key components include: tokens Token usage information. meta List response metadata (latency, node, API balance).

title: Perform a Kagi summarization in one call — kagi_summarize_once
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Summarize a URL res <- kagi_summarize_once(   url = "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZSRHeXYDLko",   engine = "muriel",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  # Summarize raw text res <- kagi_summarize_once(   text = "Long input text to be summarized...",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "takeaway" ) } # }

what: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
title: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
text: Build typed S3 object class kagi_connection holds basic configuration required talk Kagi API. includes API base URL, endpoint, authentication key, retry settings.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
code: new_kagi_connection(   base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",   endpoint = as.character(NA),   api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),   max_tries = 3 )

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
text: base_url Character scalar. Base URL Kagi API. Defaults "https://kagi.com/api/v0". endpoint Character scalar. Specific endpoint use (e.g. "search", "enrich", "summarize"). Must non-empty. api_key API key used authentication. default read environment variable KAGI_API_KEY. Best practice set variable ~/.Renviron. Advanced users may also supply function resolves key lazily request time (see resolve_api_key()). max_tries Integer scalar. Maximum number retry attempts transient errors. Defaults 3.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
text: object class kagi_connection components: base_url Base API URL. endpoint Endpoint string. api_key API key (function resolve ). max_tries Maximum retry attempts.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
text: low-level constructor; users call directly. Instead, use higher-level helpers kagi_search_once(), kagi_enrich_once(), kagi_summarize_once(), create connections internally.

title: Construct a Kagi API connection — new_kagi_connection
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic connection (API key from env var) conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search") conn  # Explicit API key conn2 <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search", api_key = "my-key")  # Lazy API key via keyring conn3 <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search",                              api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi")) } # }

what: Create a new Kagi enrich request — new_kagi_enrich
title: Create a new Kagi enrich request — new_kagi_enrich
text: Create new Kagi enrich request

title: Create a new Kagi enrich request — new_kagi_enrich
code: new_kagi_enrich(conn, q, limit = NULL)

title: Create a new Kagi enrich request — new_kagi_enrich
text: conn kagi_connection object q enrich query limit Maximum number results return

title: Create a new Kagi enrich request — new_kagi_enrich
text: kagi_enrich object

title: Construct enrich results object — new_kagi_enrich_results
text: Build typed S3 object class kagi_enrich_results successful Kagi Enrich API response. object keeps raw JSON payload parsed content tibble convenient downstream use.

title: Construct enrich results object — new_kagi_enrich_results
code: new_kagi_enrich_results(enrich, raw_json, parsed)

title: Construct enrich results object — new_kagi_enrich_results
text: enrich prepared enrich request class kagi_enrich (object passed API). raw_json length-one character string containing raw JSON response body returned API. parsed parsed list (e.g. jsonlite::fromJSON(..., simplifyVector = FALSE)) expected contain top-level elements meta data.

title: Construct enrich results object — new_kagi_enrich_results
text: object class kagi_enrich_results components: enrich original kagi_enrich request. json Raw JSON string reproducibility/debugging. meta List response metadata (e.g., id, ms, node, api_balance). data tibble enrichment hits (present). row typically corresponds enrichment result fields url, title, snippet, etc. results available, NULL.

title: Construct enrich results object — new_kagi_enrich_results
text: low-level constructor; users obtain kagi_enrich_results via enrich_perform() kagi_enrich_once(). Results coerced tibble ease inspection data manipulation. rows missing url dropped.

title: Construct enrich results object — new_kagi_enrich_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ parsed <- list(   meta = list(ms = 180, node = "def", api_balance = 95),   data = list(              list(                 t = 0,                 url = "https://example.org",                 title = "Enrich result",                 snippet = "Example..."                 )              ) ) enrich <- new_kagi_enrich(new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "enrich"), q = "open data") raw_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(parsed, auto_unbox = TRUE) res <- new_kagi_enrich_results(enrich, raw_json, parsed) res tibble::as_tibble(res$data) } # }

title: Construct search results object — new_kagi_search_results

title: Construct search results object — new_kagi_search_results
text: object class kagi_search_results components: search original kagi_search request. json Raw JSON string reproducibility/debugging. meta List response metadata (e.g., id, ms, node, api_balance). data tibble search hits (present). row typically corresponds search result fields url, title, snippet, etc. results available, NULL.

title: Construct search results object — new_kagi_search_results

title: Construct search results object — new_kagi_search_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ parsed <- list(   meta = list(ms = 200, node = "abc", api_balance = 99),   data = list(list(t = 0, url = "https://example.com", title = "Result 1", snippet = "Example...")) ) search <- new_kagi_search(new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "search"), q = "openalex api") raw_json <- jsonlite::toJSON(parsed, auto_unbox = TRUE) res <- new_kagi_search_results(search, raw_json, parsed) res tibble::as_tibble(res$data) } # }

what: Create a new Kagi summarize request — new_kagi_summarize
title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — new_kagi_summarize
text: Construct typed S3 object class kagi_summarize describes Universal Summarizer request. Use kagi_perform() execute request obtain new_kagi_summarize_results() object.

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — new_kagi_summarize
code: new_kagi_summarize(   conn,   url = NULL,   text = NULL,   engine = c("cecil", "agnes", "muriel", "daphne"),   summary_type = c("summary", "takeaway"),   target_language = c("EN", "BG", "CS", "DA", "DE", "EL", "ES", "ET", "FI", "FR", "HU",     "ID", "IT", "JA", "KO", "LT", "LV", "NB", "NL", "PL", "PT", "RO", "RU", "SK", "SL",     "SV", "TR", "UK", "ZH", "ZH-HANT"),   cache = TRUE )

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — new_kagi_summarize
text: conn kagi_connection object created new_kagi_connection(). url Optional character scalar. URL summarized. Mutually exclusive text. text Optional character scalar. Raw text summarized. Mutually exclusive url. engine Character scalar. Summarizer engine, one "cecil", "agnes", "muriel", "daphne". summary_type Character scalar. Type summary requested, one "summary" "takeaway". target_language Character scalar. Target language (ISO code "EN", "FR", "DE", …). cache Logical. Whether allow API-side caching.

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — new_kagi_summarize
text: kagi_summarize object passed kagi_perform().

title: Create a new Kagi summarize request — new_kagi_summarize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "summarize") req <- new_kagi_summarize(conn, text = "Lorem ipsum") req } # }

title: Construct summarize results object — new_kagi_summarize_results
text: Build typed S3 object class kagi_summarize_results successful Universal Summarizer response. object keeps raw JSON payload (returned API) parsed fields typically use (meta, summary, tokens).

title: Construct summarize results object — new_kagi_summarize_results
code: new_kagi_summarize_results(request, raw_json, parsed)

title: Construct summarize results object — new_kagi_summarize_results
text: request prepared summarize request class kagi_summarize (object passed API). raw_json length-one character string containing raw JSON response body returned API. parsed parsed list (e.g. jsonlite::fromJSON(..., simplifyVector = FALSE)) expected contain top-level elements meta data. data, function extracts output (summary) tokens.

title: Construct summarize results object — new_kagi_summarize_results
text: object class kagi_summarize_results components: request original kagi_summarize request. raw_json Raw JSON string reproducibility/debugging. meta List response metadata (e.g., ms, node, api_balance). summary Character scalar summarizer output text (list() absent). tokens Integer (list() absent) token usage reported API.

title: Construct summarize results object — new_kagi_summarize_results
text: low-level constructor; users obtain kagi_summarize_results via kagi_perform() (high-level wrapper). fields use %||% provide safe fallbacks API omits optional elements.

title: Construct summarize results object — new_kagi_summarize_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ parsed <- list(   meta = list(ms = 123, node = "xyz", api_balance = 42),   data = list(output = "Short summary...", tokens = 256L) ) req <- new_kagi_summarize(new_kagi_connection(endpoint = "summarize"), text = "Lorem ipsum") res <- new_kagi_summarize_results(req, raw_json = jsonlite::toJSON(parsed), parsed = parsed) res } # }
